Off Calendar May-15-2026 Continued To Sep-02-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Striking Portions Of Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Ryan Patrick; 415-552-8230; Wiegel Law Group, PLC; counsel for defendants, 888 O' Farrell G2 Partners, et al. appearing via Zoom.
Ahmad Moss; 510-981-9654; plaintiff in pro per appearing via Zoom.

Mr. Moss contests the tentative ruling. Mr. Patrick informs the court no notice was given. Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF AHMAD MOSS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Striking Portions Of Complaint
is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry its burden. Additionally, the motion is not set on proper notice and the proof of service on file does not reflect service on the counsel of record in this action.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Plaintiff Ahmad I. Moss in pro per.

No hearing held.

Plaintiff states that notice was not given to the other party.

The Court lets plaintiff know that it is a violation of due process without proper notice to the other party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GREYSTAR MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS GRAYSTAR MANAGEMENT SERVICES), KIMRAJ JORDAN, NINA KHA, LELE LONG-CHASTANG DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised by the demurrer. No opposition filed.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

To Vacate Order Granting Defendants' Demurrers
Off Calendar Mar-03-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO VACATE ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER

Appearances: Ahmad Moss (510.981.9654) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona; No other appearances.

Discussed as to the problems with the copy of the proof of service on file. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
